entries.title: The Varn System
entries.body:
The Varn system is a red dwarf system containing five known orbiting bodies. Varn itself is a red dwarf, a small, dim and long lived star.

## Orbiting Bodies

Nar-54 and Nar-55 are two small rocky worlds orbiting close to Varn, within what would be the inner system. They are tidally locked to the star and geologically inactive.

Velundra is the third planet and the only planet within Varn's habitable zone. It is the system's only inhabited world.

Val-86 is a gas giant roughly the size of Jupiter orbiting beyond Velundra. It plays no significant role in Solveyran culture or politics but it's gravitational presence has likely contributed to the systems long term stability.

Gar-84 is an ice planet at the outer edge of the system, the furthest body from Varn. It is largely unexplored.

## The Dyson Swarm

Varn is encircled by Solveyra's Dyson swarm, a vast network of energy collection infrastructure that harvests stellar output and transmits it back to Velundra via relay towers. The swarm represents the largest engineering project ever undertaken by Solveyra and it is the foundational component of the nation's energy infrastructure.

entries.title: Solveyra Overview
entries.body:
Solveyra itself is a nation that is largely unadvanced in the forms of interstellar travel, with FTL only recently being invented, however they have undergone quite a few large scale engineering projects, such as their dyson swarm with allows them to have an easy access to energy from Varn, the energy collected is then sent back and harvested by one of the few above water structures built by Solveyra, those being large energy collection towers with 90% of their actual structure being underwater in order to send the energy back to civilization.

The bulk of Solveyra is underwater, with their cities design being that similar to Eclipse punk (Search it up, I ain't explaining it), with the nation itself being ran by a democratically elected council and stretching deep into the abyss of Velundra.

Solveyra's tech itself is rather limited, as stated before FTL was only recently invented, however where they specialize is energy, primarily due to kinetics being rather difficult to use due to the water and low gravity of the planet, with most of their machinery, weaponry, and day to day life being aided significantly by the use of powerful focused beams of energy powered by their star.

However Solveyra lacks a standing military, due to their history being one of peace they never saw reason to create one, with the closest one being a standard police force that's mostly demilitarized, and they see no reason to build a military either due to the thinking that the universe is, empty.

Solveyra's population is 80 billion.

entries.title: Velundra
entries.subtitle: Homeworld
entries.body:
Velundra is a Hycean world, larger then Earth but smaller then Neptune, orbiting the red dwarf Varn with an orbit period of roughly 30 days. It is the only body in the Varn system within the habitable zone. It's mass is approximately 40% water with no exposed landmass anywhere on it's surface. The planet does have a rocky core but it sits entirely beneath the ocean with no surface expression. Velundra's gravity is approximately 1.2G.

The atmosphere is dense and hydrogen rich, composed of roughly 70% hydrogen, 20% oxygen and 10% varied gases. Surface light from Varn is dim and light penetration into the ocean is minimal. From the Shallows onwards Velundra's oceans are dark, with it being navigated by it's inhabitants using echolocation and bioluminescence.

## Temperature and Pressure

Surface ocean temperatures are relatively warm, heated by Varn's output and the insulating effect of the atmosphere. However temperatures drop significantly through the upper layers, reaching it's coldest point before beginning to heat up again in the deepest parts due to geothermal activity, however pressure at those depths are at their most extreme.

## Ocean Layers

Velundra's ocean is understood in three layers, defined by depth, pressure, temperature and the life they support.

The Surface layer spans from the ocean surface down to approximately 300km. It receives the most light of any layer, though this amounts to a dim filtered glow at best. This layer hosts the majority of Venlundra's smaller and mid-sized lifeforms. It is the most ecologically diverse layer but it is not where the Velnorca originated.

The Deep layer spans from 300km to 700km and is the largest of the three. It is cold, dark and under significant pressure. This is where the Velnorca evolved, adapted to darkness through echolocation and to the pressure through their streamlined physiology. The largest lifeforms on Velundra are found here. Solveyran cities span this layer and extend into the upper abyss.

The Abyss spans from 700km to the ocean floor. Pressure here is immense and the environment is hostile to most life. Geothermal vents warm the water and support sparse but specialized ecosystems. The Abyss is where Elenaric Crystals form, grown over geological timescales under conditions of extreme pressure. Mining operations here represent some of the most demanding engineering work undertaken by Solveyra.

## Moons

Velundra has two moons, Mar and Var, both roughly twice the size of Earth's moon. Their tidal effect on the planet is limited due to the lack of landmasses and an ocean covering the surface, tidal forces are more specified to broad slow swells rather then coastal tides as seen on terrestrial worlds.

entries.title: Solveyra's Political Ideology
entries.subtitle: Civil Republican Sortition Deliberative Democracy
entries.body:
Solveyra doesn't operate under any formal ideology, however the structure of it's government and values embed it firmly within a specific political tradition.

## Sortition

The foundation of Solveyran democracy is sortition, the selection of governing bodies are done via lottery instead of a conventional election. Citizens are not voted into power by campaign or platform, instead civilians are voted into power by their peers via a nation wide lottery, with the 8 highest regarded being seated on the council. This system was designed to prevent consolidation of political power amongst a certain class and to ensure that governance remains a civic function rather then a career. The result is that the council can include scientists, engineers, administrators, and even the common man.

## Deliberative Process

Decisions within the council do not pass quickly, with every action needing to be written as a formal proposal, submitted, voted upon and passed by a council majority before it takes effect. This is slow on purpose, as the inefficiency safeguards against impulsive or corrupt decision making and ensures that every significant action taken has been actually considered, as a result bribery and corruption within the council are effectively 0.

## Civil Republicanism

Underlying both systems is a strong sense of shared civil identity. Solveyrans broadly understand the nation's major infrastructure, the Dyson swarm, cities and energy grid, as collective endeavors belonging to the people rather then any government, corporation or private interest. Public service, including council membership, is understood as a civil service instead of a position of privilege. This cultural disposition towards collective ownership and responsibility is what holds everything together.

entries.title: Solveyra's Economic Ideology
entries.body:
## Economy

Solveyra does not have a currency or even a formal economic system in the conventional sense. Capitalism never actually emerged as the conditions that typically lead to it, such as material competition, never took hold in any meaningful way. Energy is functionally infinite courtesy of the dyson swarm, food is abundant in Velundra's oceans, and the base instinct of cooperation Velnorca hold meant that hoarding resources ran counter to the species' natural inclinations.

What exists instead is a contribution economy. Velnorca work because it is in their nature to do so, to contribute to the pod. Those who do not contribute are not punished by law but will naturally receive less in turn as the community reciprocates in turn. A Velnorca who doesn't contribute to gathering food will simply just, not receive as much as standard.

The council manages the large scale infrastructure, investment and resource allocation but does not manage individual economic activity, mainly cause there is nothing to control.

## Healthcare

Healthcare in Solveyra is universal and entirely free. There are no fees, insurance system, or administrative body overseeing the healthcare network as a whole. Hospitals and medical facilities are distributed throughout Velundra and are funded through council investment and staffed by those who chose medical work as their contribution to the pod.

## Education

Formal educational institutions in the conventional sense do not exist in Solveyra. Education is instead entirely driven by the community, with each generation being taught by the one before, passing down practical knowledge, history and civil values through direct interaction. This system predates Solveyra as a national ideal and traces it's structure back to full pod life.

The content of education varies by community and zone, reflecting the specializations needed for life in that area. Deep zone communities will naturally produce more engineers and infrastructure workers. Civil and administrative knowledge is passed down through families and pods with historical council membership. Nothing is formally standardized yet the system produces a broadly education and civically engaged populace.

## Civil Liberties

Solveyra is an extremely free society by most measures. The council does not legislate personal behavior beyond what directly affects others, and there is no cultural tradition of state interference in personal life. Velnorca are free to live, work and associate as they choose.

However freedom in Solveyra comes with an expectation of contribution. It is not a legal requirement but a social and biological one. The cooperative instinct of the Velnorca means that choosing not to contribute to the community runs against baseline instincts and those that choose to do so typically experience significant psychological distress.

## Justice System

Crime in Solveyra is rare to the point of being a statistical anomaly. Violent crime in particular is nearly nonexistent as the cooperative nature of the Velnorca makes unprovoked harm to a community member deeply counterintuitive at the biological level. The demilitarized police force that exists is primarily concerned with resolving civil matters, safety enforcement in the depth zones and the occasional individual experiencing psychological distress.

When crime does occur the response is typically rehabilitatory rather then punitive. Incarceration exists but is used sparingly and only where an individual poses a direct risk to themselves or others. The underlying philosophy is that criminal behavior done by a Velnorca is symptomatic of something having gone terribly wrong, biologically, psychologically or socially and that the correct response is to address what caused it rather then to punish the outcome.

## Attitude towards outsiders

Velnorca approach non Velnorca with a combination of intense curiosity and significant caution. First contact with an alien civilization is a profound moment for Solveyra specifically because other intelligent life had not been seriously considered with the assumption being that Velundra was unique.

Worth noting however is a biological factor that Velnorca are generally candid about. The predatory instincts of the species means that beings smaller then them trigger a low level prey response. This instinct is considered controllable and Velnorca are not considered dangerous on this basis. Velnorca in diplomatic or interspecies contexts are trained to manage this response and most find it manageable with minimal effort. It is considered poor mannered to mention it unprompted but not a subject of shame.
